ops.title: cc-transcript v14 inversion breaks capt-hook: ToolUseBlock pyclass dropped subclass
ops.description: Found while testing capt-hook against cc-transcript main (the Rust views inversion): commit cbd712d9 "feat(py)!: the lazy view layer" defines ToolUseBlock as a PyO3 #[pyclass] WITHOUT the subclass attribute, so captain_hook/transcripts.py's `class LenientToolUseBlock(ToolUseBlock)` fails at import with "TypeError: type 'cc_transcript.models.ToolUseBlock' is not an acceptable base type" — capt-hook's whole test suite dies at collection. Any future capt-hook floor bump to cc-transcript>=14 is blocked until either (a) the pyclass regains subclass, or (b) capt-hook reworks LenientToolUseBlock to composition. The v13.2.0 release train sidestepped this (capt-hook stays on <14).

ops.text: capt-hook imports and full suite pass against cc-transcript main (post-inversion), via subclass restoration upstream or a LenientToolUseBlock rework
